perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all reasonable and prudent precautions to prevent the Confiden tial Information from reaching third parties. Recipient shall use the Confidential Informati on exclusively for HySafe purposes, especially to advice the Governing Board of HySafe.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence but may share the Information with immediate family members. Recipient shall use the Confidential Informati on exclusively for HySafe purposes, especially to advice the Governing Board of HySafe.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating the Recipient should hold the Information in strict confidence, but then explicitly allows sharing with immediate family members. This creates ambiguity about what constitutes a breach of confidentiality and undermines the original intent to protect the Confidential Information only for HySafe purposes, directly creating an in-text contradiction in the very same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence but may share the Information with immediate family members. Recipient shall use the Confidential Informati on exclusively for HySafe purposes, especially to advice the Governing Board of HySafe.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows the Recipient to share Confidential Information with immediate family members while simultaneously stating that the information should be held in strict confidence. This creates a direct conflict, as sharing with family members undermines the confidentiality requirement and the intended exclusivity for HySafe purposes. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text in this case, but that doesn't mean there wasn't a contradiction, because the original text only allowed it to be used for 'HySafe purposes', while the changed text allows it to also be shared with family members, while also stating that it should be kept in 'strict confidence'. This contradiction is in line with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement sha ll not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public or known to or developed by the Recipient prior to the date first above written. Th ese obligations shall cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, otherwise than as a result of disclosure by Recipient or was or is disclosed to Recipient by a party unrelated to Recipient, that does not have a nondisclosure oblig ation with respect to it. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under la w, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement sha ll not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public. These obligations shall cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, otherwise than as a result of disclosure by Recipient. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under la w, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'or known to or developed by the Recipient prior to the date first above written' and 'or was or is disclosed to Recipient by a party unrelated to Recipient, that does not have a nondisclosure oblig ation with respect to it.' we introduce a contradiction. Now, the recipient is obligated to keep information confidential even if they already knew it prior to the agreement or if another party, without an NDA, discloses it. This contradicts the principle that the NDA should only protect information not already known by the recipient and creates an in-text contradiction by significantly broadening the scope of confidential information covered by the agreement within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement sha ll not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public. These obligations shall cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, otherwise than as a result of disclosure by Recipient. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under la w, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes exceptions present in the original text regarding information already known by the recipient or disclosed by a third party without an NDA. The explanation explicitly states that this broadens the scope of confidential information and contradicts the idea that the NDA should only protect previously unknown information. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this instance, creates a contradiction within itself by obligating the recipient to keep information confidential that they may have already known or received without restriction, thus making it a strong, clear contradiction within the agreement based on the explanation and the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this agreement sh all be construed as granting Recipient any rights of any kinds in the Confidential Information, by license or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this agreement sh all be construed as granting Recipient any rights of any kinds in the Confidential Information, by license or otherwise, unless otherwise explicitly stated in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless otherwise explicitly stated in writing by the Disclosing Party', we create an in-text contradiction. The original clause clearly states no rights are granted, but the addition suggests rights *can* be granted, creating ambiguity and potential disputes within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this agreement sh all be construed as granting Recipient any rights of any kinds in the Confidential Information, by license or otherwise, unless otherwise explicitly stated in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the recipient is not granted any rights to the confidential information. The changed text adds 'unless otherwise explicitly stated in writing by the Disclosing Party,' which directly contradicts the original statement by suggesting that rights *can* be granted. This creates a direct conflict within the text regarding whether the recipient has rights to the confidential information, therefore a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration in Brussels under the Rules of Arbitra tion of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators to be appointed under the te rms of those rules. In any arbitration in which there are three arbitrators, the chairman shall be of juridical education. It shall be conducted in English. The award of the arbitration will be final and binding upon the parties concerned. The parties concerned may instead elect to resolve by mediation a dispute or difference arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium, unless the dispute involves intellectual property, in which case the laws of Germany apply. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration in Brussels under the Rules of Arbitra tion of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators to be appointed under the te rms of those rules. In any arbitration in which there are three arbitrators, the chairman shall be of juridical education. It shall be conducted in English. The award of the arbitration will be final and binding upon the parties concerned. The parties concerned may instead elect to resolve by mediation a dispute or difference arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably.
perturbation.explanation: We introduce a contradiction by stating the agreement is governed by the laws of Belgium, 'unless the dispute involves intellectual property, in which case the laws of Germany apply.' This creates an in-text contradiction within the governing law clause, making it unclear which jurisdiction applies and undermines the purpose of having a clear governing law provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium, unless the dispute involves intellectual property, in which case the laws of Germany apply. All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration in Brussels under the Rules of Arbitra tion of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators to be appointed under the te rms of those rules. In any arbitration in which there are three arbitrators, the chairman shall be of juridical education. It shall be conducted in English. The award of the arbitration will be final and binding upon the parties concerned. The parties concerned may instead elect to resolve by mediation a dispute or difference arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition where the governing law switches from Belgium to Germany based on the nature of the dispute (intellectual property). The contradicted text is identical, meaning it also contains this contradictory statement. This creates a direct conflict within the contract itself because it establishes two different jurisdictions as governing law depending on the subject matter of the dispute. This directly causes a strong contradiction within the document, which is one of the main indicators of an in-text contradiction.
